No matter who you are, if you bring strong emotions near this Pokémon, it will silence you violently.
It emits psychic power strong enough to cause headaches as a deterrent to the approach of others.
Through its nose, it sucks in the emanations produced by people and Pokémon when they feel annoyed. It thrives off this negative energy.
When it gets down on all fours as if to beg for forgiveness, it’s trying to lure opponents in so that it can stab them with its spear-like hair.
With the hair wrapped around its body helping to enhance its muscles, this Pokémon can overwhelm even Machamp.
Its voice is staggering in volume. Obstagoon has a tendency to take on a threatening posture and shout—this move is known as Obstruct.
What appears to be an iron helmet is actually hardened hair. This Pokémon lives for the thrill of battle.
Its shell is overflowing with its heightened otherworldly energy. The ectoplasm serves as protection for this Pokémon’s core spirit.
Only Farfetch’d that have survived many battles can attain this evolution. When this Pokémon’s leek withers, it will retire from combat.
It’s highly skilled at tap-dancing. It waves its cane of ice in time with its graceful movements.
A powerful curse was woven into an ancient painting. After absorbing the spirit of a Yamask, the painting began to move.
This Pokémon was born from sweet-smelling particles in the air. Its body is made of cream.
When it trusts a Trainer, it will treat them to berries it’s decorated with cream.
Five of them are troopers, and one is the brass. The brass’s orders are absolute.
It feeds on seaweed, using its teeth to scrape it off rocks. Electric current flows from the tips of its spines.
It spits out thread imbued with a frigid sort of energy and uses it to tie its body to branches, disguising itself as an icicle while it sleeps.
Icy scales fall from its wings like snow as it flies over fields and mountains. The temperature of its wings is less than −290 degrees Fahrenheit.
It stands in grasslands, watching the sun’s descent from zenith to horizon. This Pokémon has a talent for delivering dynamic kicks.
It drifted in on the flow of ocean waters from a frigid place. It keeps its head iced constantly to make sure it stays nice and cold.
It uses the horns on its head to sense the emotions of others. Males will act as valets for those they serve, looking after their every need.
As it eats the seeds stored up in its pocket-like pouches, this Pokémon is not just satisfying its constant hunger. It’s also generating electricity.
It digs up the ground with its trunk. It’s also very strong, being able to carry loads of over five tons without any problem at all.
They came over from another region long ago and worked together with humans. Their green skin is resistant to water.
In ancient times, it was unbeatable thanks to its powerful lower body, but it went extinct anyway after it depleted all its plant-based food sources.
The shaking of its freezing upper half is what generates its electricity. It has a hard time walking around.
Powerful legs and jaws made it the apex predator of its time. Its own overhunting of its prey was what drove it to extinction.
Though it’s able to capture prey by freezing its surroundings, it has trouble eating the prey afterward because its mouth is on top of its head.
Its body resembles polished metal, and it’s both lightweight and strong. The only drawback is that it rusts easily.
After being reborn as a ghost Pokémon, Dreepy wanders the areas it used to inhabit back when it was alive in prehistoric seas.
It’s capable of flying faster than 120 mph. It battles alongside Dreepy and dotes on them until they successfully evolve.
When it isn’t battling, it keeps Dreepy in the holes on its horns. Once a fight starts, it launches the Dreepy like supersonic missiles.
Known as a legendary hero, this Pokémon absorbs metal particles, transforming them into a weapon it uses to battle.
In times past, it worked together with a king of the people to save the Galar region. It absorbs metal that it then uses in battle.
The core on its chest absorbs energy emanating from the lands of the Galar region. This energy is what allows Eternatus to stay active.
Kubfu trains hard to perfect its moves. The moves it masters will determine which form it takes when it evolves.
This form of Urshifu is a strong believer in the one-hit KO. Its strategy is to leap in close to foes and land a devastating blow with a hardened fist.
Within dense forests, this Pokémon lives in a pack with others of its kind. It’s incredibly aggressive, and the other Pokémon of the forest fear it.
This Pokémon is a cluster of electrical energy. It’s said that removing the rings on Regieleki’s body will unleash the Pokémon’s latent power.
An academic theory proposes that Regidrago’s arms were once the head of an ancient dragon Pokémon. The theory remains unproven.
Glastrier emits intense cold from its hooves. It’s also a belligerent Pokémon—anything it wants, it takes by force.
It probes its surroundings with all its senses save one—it doesn’t use its sense of sight. Spectrier’s kicks are said to separate soul from body.
Calyrex is a merciful Pokémon, capable of providing healing and blessings. It reigned over the Galar region in times of yore.
The black orbs shine with an uncanny light when the Pokémon is erecting invisible barriers. The fur shed from its beard retains heat well and is a highly useful material for winter clothing.
A violent creature that fells towering trees with its crude axes and shields itself with hard stone. If one should chance upon this Pokémon in the wilds, one's only recourse is to flee.
I believe it was Hisui's swampy terrain that gave Ursaluna its burly physique and newfound capacity to manipulate peat at will.
Clads itself in the souls of comrades that perished before fulfilling their goals of journeying upstream. No other species throughout all Hisui's rivers is Basculegion's equal.
Because of Sneasler's virulent poison and daunting physical prowess, no other species could hope to best it on the frozen highlands. Preferring solitude, this species does not form packs.
Its lancelike spikes and savage temperament have earned it the nickname ”sea fiend.” It slurps up poison to nourish itself.
When it flies to this land from across the sea, the bitter winter comes to an end. According to legend, this Pokémon's love gives rise to the budding of fresh life across Hisui.
Its fluffy fur is similar in composition to plants. This Pokémon frequently washes its face to keep it from drying out.
Floragato deftly wields the vine hidden beneath its long fur, slamming the hard flower bud against its opponents.
This Pokémon uses the reflective fur lining its cape to camouflage the stem of its flower, creating the illusion that the flower is floating.
It lies on warm rocks and uses the heat absorbed by its square-shaped scales to create fire energy.
The combination of Crocalor’s fire energy and overflowing vitality has caused an egg-shaped fireball to appear on the Pokémon’s head.
The fiery bird changes shape when Skeledirge sings. Rumor has it that the bird was born when the fireball on Skeledirge’s head gained a soul.
This Pokémon migrated to Paldea from distant lands long ago. The gel secreted by its feathers repels water and grime."
These Pokémon constantly run through shallow waters to train their legs, then compete with each other to see which of them kicks most gracefully.
A single kick from a Quaquaval can send a truck rolling. This Pokémon uses its powerful legs to perform striking dances from far-off lands.
It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn’t use it for anything other than foraging.
Oinkologne is proud of its fine, glossy skin. It emits a concentrated scent from the tip of its tail.
The ball of threads wrapped around its body is elastic enough to deflect the scythes of Scyther, this Pokémon’s natural enemy.
It clings to branches and ceilings using its threads and moves without a sound. It takes out its prey before the prey even notices it.
It has its third set of legs folded up. When it’s in a tough spot, this Pokémon jumps over 30 feet using the strength of its legs.
When it decides to fight all out, it stands on its previously folded legs to enter Showdown Mode. It neutralizes its enemies in short order.
It has underdeveloped electric sacs on its cheeks. These sacs can produce electricity only if Pawmi rubs them furiously with the pads on its forepaws.
When its group is attacked, Pawmo is the first to leap into battle, defeating enemies with a fighting technique that utilizes electric shocks.
This Pokémon normally is slow to react, but once it enters battle, it will strike down its enemies with lightning-fast movements.
Exhibiting great teamwork, they use their incisors to cut pieces out of any material that might be useful for a nest, then make off with them.
The two little ones just appeared one day. The group might be a family of related Pokémon, but nobody knows for sure.
This Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough’s breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon’s vicinity.
The pleasant aroma that emanates from this Pokémon’s body helps wheat grow, so Dachsbun has been treasured by farming villages.
It protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.
Dolliv shares its tasty, fresh-scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.
This calm Pokémon is very compassionate. It will share its delicious, nutrient-rich oil with weakened Pokémon.
These Pokémon prefer to live in cities. They form flocks based on the color of their feathers, and they fight over territory.
It was born in a layer of rock salt deep under the earth. This species was particularly treasured in the old days, as they would share precious salt.
This Pokémon dry cures its prey by spraying salt over them. The curing process steals away the water in the prey’s body.
Garganacl will rub its fingertips together and sprinkle injured Pokémon with salt. Even severe wounds will promptly heal afterward.
Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.
Armarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal.
The fiery blades on its arms burn fiercely with the lingering resentment of a sword wielder who fell before accomplishing their goal.
Tadbulb shakes its tail to generate electricity. If it senses danger, it will make its head blink on and off to alert its allies.
When this Pokémon expands and contracts its wobbly body, the belly-button dynamo in its stomach produces a huge amount of electricity.
When its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.
Kilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.
It always scowls in an attempt to make opponents take it seriously, but even crying children will burst into laughter when they see Maschiff’s face.
This Pokémon can store energy in its large dewlap. Mabosstiff unleashes this energy all at once to blow away enemies.
Though usually a mellow Pokémon, it will sink its sharp, poison-soaked front teeth into any that anger it, causing paralysis in the object of its ire.
The color of the poisonous saliva depends on what the Pokémon eats. Grafaiai covers its fingers in its saliva and draws patterns on trees in forests.
A soul unable to move on to the afterlife was blown around by the wind until it got tangled up with dried grass and became a Pokémon.
It will open the branches of its head to envelop its prey. Once it absorbs all the life energy it needs, it expels the prey and discards it.
Toedscool lives in muggy forests. The flaps that fall from its body are chewy and very delicious.
These Pokémon gather into groups and form colonies deep within forests. They absolutely hate it when strangers approach.
Klawf hangs upside-down from cliffs, waiting for prey. But Klawf can’t remain in this position for long because its blood rushes to its head.
The more sunlight this Pokémon bathes in, the more spicy chemicals are produced by its body, and thus the spicier its moves become.
The red head converts spicy chemicals into fire energy and blasts the surrounding area with a super spicy stream of flame.
This Pokémon creates a mud ball by mixing sand and dirt with psychic energy. It treasures its mud ball more than its own life.
The body that supports the ball barely moves. Therefore, it is thought that the true body of this Pokémon is actually inside the ball.
Flittle’s toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon’s belly.
It immobilizes opponents by bathing them in psychic power from its large eyes. Despite its appearance, it has a vicious temperament.
It swings its handmade hammer around to protect itself, but the hammer is often stolen by Pokémon that eat metal.
This Pokémon will attack groups of Pawniard and Bisharp, gathering metal from them in order to create a large and sturdy hammer.
This intelligent Pokémon has a very daring disposition. It knocks rocks into the sky with its hammer, aiming for flying Corviknight.
This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.
It has a vicious temperament, contrary to what its appearance may suggest. It wraps its long bodies around prey, then drags the prey into its den.
It gathers things up in an apron made from shed feathers added to the Pokémon’s chest feathers, then drops those things from high places for fun.
It likes playing with others of its kind using the water ring on its tail. It uses ultrasonic waves to sense the emotions of other living creatures.
This Pokémon changes its appearance if it hears its allies calling for help. Palafin will never show anybody its moment of transformation.
It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.
It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders—now numbering eight—to generate energy.
Apparently Cyclizar has been allowing people to ride on its back since ancient times. Depictions of this have been found in 10,000-year-old murals.
When attacked, this Pokémon will wield the tendrils on its body like fists and pelt the opponent with a storm of punches.
It absorbs nutrients from cave walls. The petals it wears are made of crystallized poison.
When this Pokémon detects danger, it will open up its crystalline petals and fire beams from its conical body.
It is said that a dog Pokémon that died in the wild without ever interacting with a human was reborn as this Pokémon.
Houndstone spends most of its time sleeping in graveyards. Among all the dog Pokémon, this one is most loyal to its master.
This Pokémon apparently ties the base of its neck into a knot so that energy stored in its belly does not escape from its beak.
This species left the ocean and began living on land a very long time ago. It seems to be closely related to Wailmer.
This Pokémon wanders around snowy, icy areas. It protects its body with powerful muscles and a thick layer of fat under its skin.
When Veluza discards unnecessary flesh, its mind becomes honed and its psychic power increases. The spare flesh has a mild but delicious flavor.
This Pokémon is a glutton, but it’s bad at getting food. It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.
This is a small dragon Pokémon. It lives inside the mouth of Dondozo to protect itself from enemies on the outside.
When its anger rose beyond a critical point, this Pokémon gained power that is unfettered by the limits of its physical body.
When attacked, this Pokémon will retaliate by sticking thick spines out from its body. It’s a risky move that puts everything on the line.
Now that the brain waves from the head and tail are synced up, the psychic power of this Pokémon is 10 times stronger than Girafarig’s.
This Pokémon uses its hard tail to make its nest by boring holes into bedrock deep underground. The nest can reach lengths of over six miles.
Only a Bisharp that stands above all others in its vast army can evolve into Kingambit.
Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. The name Great Tusk was taken from a creature listed in a certain book.
There has been only one reported sighting of this Pokémon. It resembles a mysterious creature depicted in an old expedition journal.
It is possible that the creature listed as Brute Bonnet in a certain book could actually be this Pokémon.
This Pokémon has characteristics similar to those of Flutter Mane, a creature mentioned in a certain book.
This mysterious Pokémon has some similarities to a creature that an old book introduced as Slither Wing.
No records exist of this Pokémon being caught. Data is lacking, but the Pokémon’s traits match up with a creature shown in an expedition journal.
This Pokémon closely resembles a scientific weapon that a paranormal magazine claimed was sent to this planet by aliens.
Its shape is similar to a robot featured in a paranormal magazine article. The robot was said to have been created by an ancient civilization.
It is very similar to a cyborg covered exclusively by a paranormal magazine. The cyborg was said to be the modified form of a certain athlete.
It resembles a certain Pokémon introduced in a paranormal magazine, described as the offspring of a Hydreigon that fell in love with a robot.
This Pokémon resembles an unknown object described in a paranormal magazine as a UFO sent to observe humanity.
It has some similarities to a Pokémon introduced in a dubious magazine as a Tyranitar from one billion years into the future.
Frigibax absorbs heat through its dorsal fin and converts the heat into ice energy. The higher the temperature, the more energy Frigibax stores.
Arctibax freezes the air around it, protecting its face with an ice mask and turning its dorsal fin into a blade of ice.
This Pokémon blasts cryogenic air out from its mouth. This air can instantly freeze even liquid-hot lava.
This Pokémon was born inside a treasure chest about 1,500 years ago. It sucks the life-force out of scoundrels who try to steal the treasure.
Its body seems to be made up of 1,000 coins. This Pokémon gets along well with others and is quick to make friends with anybody.
The grudge of a person punished for writing the king’s evil deeds upon wooden tablets has clad itself in dead leaves to become a Pokémon.
This Pokémon can control 100 tons of fallen snow. It plays around innocently by leaping in and out of avalanches it has caused.
The fear poured into an ancient ritual vessel has clad itself in rocks and dirt to become a Pokémon.
It controls flames burning at over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit. It casually swims through the sea of lava it creates by melting rock and sand.
It is possible that this is the creature listed as Roaring Moon in an expedition journal that still holds many mysteries.
It has some similarities to a mad scientist’s invention covered in a paranormal magazine.
This seems to be the Winged King mentioned in an old expedition journal. It was said to have split the land with its bare fists.
Much remains unknown about this creature. It resembles Cyclizar, but it is far more ruthless and powerful.
This ferocious creature is shrouded in mystery. It's named after an aquatic monster mentioned in an old expedition journal.
Many of its physical characteristics match those of a Virizion from the future that was covered in a paranormal magazine.
Dipplin is two creatures in one Pokémon. Its evolution was triggered by a special apple grown only in one place.
Supposedly, the regrets of a tea ceremony master who died before perfecting his craft lingered in some matcha and became a Pokémon.
It pretends to be tea, trying to fool people into drinking it so it can drain their life-force. Its ruse is generally unsuccessful.
After all its muscles were stimulated by the toxic chain around its neck, Okidogi transformed and gained a powerful physique.
The chain is made from toxins that enhance capabilities. It stimulated Munkidori's brain and caused the Pokémon's psychic powers to bloom.
Fezandipiti owes its beautiful looks and lovely voice to the toxic stimulants emanating from the chain wrapped around its body.
This Pokémon's type changes based on which mask it's wearing. It confounds its enemies with nimble movements and kicks.
It gathers static electricity from its surroundings. The beams it launches when down on all fours are tremendously powerful.
Seven syrpents live inside an apple made of syrup. The syrpent in the center is the commander.
There are scant few reports of this creature being sighted. One short video shows it rampaging and spouting pillars of flame.
It's said to incinerate everything around it with lightning launched from its fur. Very little is known about this creature.
It resembles a Pokémon described in a dubious magazine as a Terrakion that had been modified by an evil organization.
It resembles a mysterious object introduced in a paranormal magazine as a cutting-edge weapon shaped like a Cobalion.
Terapagos protects itself using its power to transform energy into hard crystals. This Pokémon is the source of the Terastal phenomenon.
It feeds others toxic mochi that draw out desires and capabilities. Those who eat the mochi fall under Pecharunt’s control, chained to its will.

fields.name: mud-bomb
fields.description: The user launches a hard-packed mud ball to attack. It may also lower the target’s accuracy.

fields.name: psycho-cut
fields.description: The user tears at the foe with blades formed by psychic power. It has a high critical-hit ratio.

fields.name: zen-headbutt
fields.description: The user focuses its willpower to its head and rams the foe. It may also make the target flinch.

fields.name: mirror-shot
fields.description: The user looses a flash of energy from its polished body. It may also lower the target’s accuracy.

fields.name: flash-cannon
fields.description: The user gathers all its light energy and releases it at once. It may also lower the foe’s Sp. Def stat.

fields.name: rock-climb
fields.description: A charging attack that may also leave the foe confused. It can also be used to scale rocky walls.

fields.name: defog
fields.description: Obstacles are moved, reducing the foe’s evasion stat. It can also be used to clear deep fog, etc.

fields.name: trick-room
fields.description: The user creates a bizarre area in which slower Pokémon get to move first for five turns.

fields.name: draco-meteor
fields.description: Comets are summoned down from the sky. The attack’s recoil sharply reduces the user’s Sp. Atk stat.

fields.name: discharge
fields.description: A flare of electricity is loosed to strike all Pokémon in battle. It may also cause paralysis.

fields.name: lava-plume
fields.description: An inferno of scarlet flames washes over all Pokémon in battle. It may also inflict burns.

fields.name: leaf-storm
fields.description: A storm of sharp leaves is whipped up. The attack’s recoil sharply reduces the user’s Sp. Atk stat.

fields.name: power-whip
fields.description: The user violently whirls its vines or tentacles to harshly lash the foe.

fields.name: rock-wrecker
fields.description: The user launches a huge boulder at the foe to attack. It must rest on the next turn, however.

fields.name: cross-poison
fields.description: A slashing attack that may also leave the target poisoned. It has a high critical-hit ratio.

fields.name: gunk-shot
fields.description: The user shoots filthy garbage at the foe to attack. It may also poison the target.

fields.name: iron-head
fields.description: The foe slams the target with its steel-hard head. It may also make the target flinch.

fields.name: magnet-bomb
fields.description: The user launches a steel bomb that sticks to the target. This attack will not miss.

fields.name: stone-edge
fields.description: The user stabs the foe with a sharpened stone. It has a high critical-hit ratio.

fields.name: captivate
fields.description: If it is the opposite gender of the user, the foe is charmed into sharply lowering its Sp. Atk stat.

fields.name: stealth-rock
fields.description: The user lays a trap of levitating stones around the foe. The trap hurts foes that switch into battle.

fields.name: grass-knot
fields.description: The user snares the foe with grass and trips it. The heavier the foe, the greater the damage.

fields.name: chatter
fields.description: The user attacks using a sound wave based on words it has learned. It may also confuse the foe.

fields.name: judgment
fields.description: The user releases countless shots of light. Its type varies with the kind of Plate the user is holding.

fields.name: bug-bite
fields.description: The user bites the foe. If the foe is holding a Berry, the user eats it and gains its effect.

fields.name: charge-beam
fields.description: The user fires a concentrated bundle of electricity. It may also raise the user’s Sp. Atk stat.

fields.name: wood-hammer
fields.description: The user slams its rugged body into the foe to attack. The user also sustains serious damage.

fields.name: aqua-jet
fields.description: The user lunges at the foe at a speed that makes it almost invisible. It is sure to strike first.

fields.name: attack-order
fields.description: The user calls out its underlings to pummel the target. Critical hits land more easily.

fields.name: defend-order
fields.description: The user calls out its underlings to make a living shield, raising its Defense and Sp. Def stats.

fields.name: heal-order
fields.description: The user calls out its underlings to heal it. The user regains up to half of its max HP.

fields.name: head-smash
fields.description: The user delivers a life-endangering head butt at full power. The user also takes terrible damage.

fields.name: double-hit
fields.description: The user slams the foe with a tail, etc. The target is hit twice in a row.

fields.name: roar-of-time
fields.description: The user blasts the foe with power that distorts even time. The user must rest on the next turn.

fields.name: spacial-rend
fields.description: The user tears the foe along with the space around it. This move has a high critical-hit ratio.

fields.name: lunar-dance
fields.description: The user faints. In return, the Pokémon taking its place will have its status and HP fully restored.

fields.name: crush-grip
fields.description: The foe is crushed with great force. The attack is more powerful the more HP the foe has left.

fields.name: magma-storm
fields.description: The foe becomes trapped within a maelstrom of fire that rages for two to five turns.

fields.name: dark-void
fields.description: The foe is dragged into a world of total darkness that puts it to sleep.

fields.name: seed-flare
fields.description: The user generates a shock wave from within its body. It may also lower the target’s Sp. Def.

fields.name: ominous-wind
fields.description: The user creates a gust of repulsive wind. It may also raise all the user’s stats at once.

fields.name: shadow-force
fields.description: The user disappears, then strikes the foe on the second turn. It hits even if the foe used Protect.

fields.name: hone-claws
fields.description: The user sharpens its claws to boost its Attack stat and accuracy.

fields.name: wide-guard
fields.description: The user and its allies are protected from wide-ranging attacks for one turn. If used in succession, its chance of failing rises.

fields.name: guard-split
fields.description: The user employs its psychic power to average its Defense and Sp. Def stats with those of its target’s.

fields.name: power-split
fields.description: The user employs its psychic power to average its Attack and Sp. Atk stats with those of the target’s.

fields.name: wonder-room
fields.description: The user creates a bizarre area in which Pokémon’s Defense and Sp. Def stats are swapped for five turns.

fields.name: psyshock
fields.description: The user materializes an odd psychic wave to attack the target. This attack does physical damage.

fields.name: venoshock
fields.description: The user drenches the target in a special poisonous liquid. Its power is doubled if the target is poisoned.

fields.name: autotomize
fields.description: The user sheds part of its body to make itself lighter and sharply raise its Speed stat.

fields.name: rage-powder
fields.description: The user scatters a cloud of irritating powder to draw attention to itself. Opponents aim only at the user.

fields.name: telekinesis
fields.description: The user makes the target float with its psychic power. The target is easier to hit for three turns.

fields.name: magic-room
fields.description: The user creates a bizarre area in which Pokémon’s held items lose their effects for five turns.

fields.name: smack-down
fields.description: The user throws a stone or projectile to attack an opponent. A flying Pokémon will fall to the ground when hit.

fields.name: storm-throw
fields.description: The user strikes the target with a fierce blow. This attack always results in a critical hit.

fields.name: flame-burst
fields.description: The user attacks the target with a bursting flame. The bursting flame damages Pokémon next to the target as well.

fields.name: sludge-wave
fields.description: It swamps the area around the user with a giant sludge wave. It may also poison those hit.

fields.name: quiver-dance
fields.description: The user lightly performs a beautiful, mystic dance. It boosts the user’s Sp. Atk, Sp. Def, and Speed stats.

fields.name: heavy-slam
fields.description: The user slams into the target with its heavy body. The more the user outweighs the target, the greater its damage.

fields.name: synchronoise
fields.description: Using an odd shock wave, the user inflicts damage on any Pokémon of the same type in the area around it.

fields.name: electro-ball
fields.description: The user hurls an electric orb at the target. The faster the user is than the target, the greater the damage.

fields.name: soak
fields.description: The user shoots a torrent of water at the target and changes the target’s type to Water.

fields.name: flame-charge
fields.description: The user cloaks itself with flame and attacks. Building up more power, it raises the user’s Speed stat.

fields.name: coil
fields.description: The user coils up and concentrates. This raises its Attack and Defense stats as well as its accuracy.

fields.name: low-sweep
fields.description: The user attacks the target’s legs swiftly, reducing the target’s Speed stat.

fields.name: acid-spray
fields.description: The user spits fluid that works to melt the target. This harshly reduces the target’s Sp. Def stat.

fields.name: foul-play
fields.description: The user turns the target’s power against it. The higher the target’s Attack stat, the greater the damage.

fields.name: simple-beam
fields.description: The user’s mysterious psychic wave changes the target’s Ability to Simple.

fields.name: entrainment
fields.description: The user dances with an odd rhythm that compels the target to mimic it, making the target’s Ability the same as the user’s.

fields.name: after-you
fields.description: The user helps the target and makes it use its move right after the user.

fields.name: round
fields.description: The user attacks the target with a song. Others can join in the Round and make the attack do greater damage.

fields.name: echoed-voice
fields.description: The user attacks the target with an echoing voice. If this move is used every turn, it does greater damage.

fields.name: chip-away
fields.description: Looking for an opening, the user strikes continually. The target’s stat changes don’t affect this attack’s damage.

fields.name: clear-smog
fields.description: The user attacks by throwing a clump of special mud. All status changes are returned to normal.

fields.name: stored-power
fields.description: The user attacks the target with stored power. The more the user’s stats are raised, the greater the damage.

fields.name: quick-guard
fields.description: The user protects itself and its allies from priority moves. If used in succession, its chance of failing rises.

fields.name: ally-switch
fields.description: The user teleports using a strange power and switches its place with one of its allies.

fields.name: scald
fields.description: The user shoots boiling hot water at its target. It may also leave the target with a burn.

fields.name: shell-smash
fields.description: The user breaks its shell, lowering its Defense and Sp. Def stats but sharply raising Attack, Sp. Atk, and Speed stats.

fields.name: heal-pulse
fields.description: The user emits a healing pulse which restores the target’s HP by up to half of its max HP.

fields.name: hex
fields.description: This relentless attack does massive damage to a target affected by status problems.

fields.name: sky-drop
fields.description: The user takes the target into the sky, then drops it during the next turn. The target cannot attack while in the sky.

fields.name: shift-gear
fields.description: The user rotates its gears, raising its Attack and sharply raising its Speed.

fields.name: circle-throw
fields.description: The user throws the target and drags out another Pokémon in its party. In the wild, the battle ends.

fields.name: incinerate
fields.description: The user attacks the target with fire. If the target is holding a Berry, the Berry becomes burnt up and unusable.

fields.name: quash
fields.description: The user suppresses the target and makes its move go last.

fields.name: acrobatics
fields.description: The user nimbly strikes the target. If the user is not holding an item, this attack inflicts massive damage.

fields.name: reflect-type
fields.description: The user reflects the target’s type, making it the same type as the target.

fields.name: retaliate
fields.description: The user gets revenge for a fainted ally. If an ally fainted in the previous turn, this attack’s damage increases.

fields.name: final-gambit
fields.description: The user risks everything to attack its target. The user faints but does damage equal to the user’s HP.

fields.name: bestow
fields.description: The user passes its held item to the target when the target isn’t holding an item.

fields.name: inferno
fields.description: The user attacks by engulfing the target in an intense fire. It leaves the target with a burn.

fields.name: water-pledge
fields.description: A column of water strikes the target. When combined with its fire equivalent, the damage increases and a rainbow appears.

fields.name: fire-pledge
fields.description: A column of fire hits opposing Pokémon. When used with its Grass equivalent, its damage increases into a vast sea of fire.

fields.name: grass-pledge
fields.description: A column of grass hits opposing Pokémon. When used with its water equivalent, its damage increases into a vast swamp.

fields.name: volt-switch
fields.description: After making its attack, the user rushes back to switch places with a party Pokémon in waiting.

fields.name: struggle-bug
fields.description: While resisting, the user attacks the opposing Pokémon. The targets’ Sp. Atk stat is reduced.

fields.name: bulldoze
fields.description: The user stomps down on the ground and attacks everything in the area. Hit Pokémon’s Speed stat is reduced.

fields.name: frost-breath
fields.description: The user blows a cold breath on the target. This attack always results in a critical hit.

fields.name: dragon-tail
fields.description: The user knocks away the target and drags out another Pokémon in its party. In the wild, the battle ends.

fields.name: work-up
fields.description: The user is roused, and its Attack and Sp. Atk stats increase.

fields.name: electroweb
fields.description: The user captures and attacks opposing Pokémon by using an electric net. It reduces the targets’ Speed stat.

fields.name: wild-charge
fields.description: The user shrouds itself in electricity and smashes into its target. It also damages the user a little.

fields.name: drill-run
fields.description: The user crashes into its target while rotating its body like a drill. Critical hits land more easily.

fields.name: dual-chop
fields.description: The user attacks its target by hitting it with brutal strikes. The target is hit twice in a row.

fields.name: heart-stamp
fields.description: The user unleashes a vicious blow after its cute act makes the target less wary. It may also make the target flinch.

fields.name: horn-leech
fields.description: The user drains the target’s energy with its horns. The user’s HP is restored by half the damage taken by the target.

fields.name: sacred-sword
fields.description: The user attacks by slicing with its long horns. The target’s stat changes don’t affect this attack’s damage.

fields.name: razor-shell
fields.description: The user cuts its target with sharp shells. This attack may also lower the target’s Defense stat.

fields.name: heat-crash
fields.description: The user slams its target with its flame- covered body. The more the user outweighs the target, the greater the damage.

fields.name: leaf-tornado
fields.description: The user attacks its target by encircling it in sharp leaves. This attack may also lower the target’s accuracy.

fields.name: steamroller
fields.description: The user crushes its targets by rolling over them with its rolled-up body. This attack may make the target flinch.

fields.name: cotton-guard
fields.description: The user protects itself by wrapping its body in soft cotton, drastically raising the user’s Defense stat.

fields.name: night-daze
fields.description: The user lets loose a pitch-black shock wave at its target. It may also lower the target’s accuracy.

fields.name: psystrike
fields.description: The user materializes an odd psychic wave to attack the target. This attack does physical damage.

fields.name: tail-slap
fields.description: The user attacks by striking the target with its hard tail. It hits the target two to five times in a row.

fields.name: hurricane
fields.description: The user attacks by wrapping its opponent in a fierce wind that flies up into the sky. It may also confuse the target.

fields.name: head-charge
fields.description: The user charges its head into its target, using its powerful guard hair. It also damages the user a little.

fields.name: gear-grind
fields.description: The user attacks by throwing two steel gears at its target.

fields.name: searing-shot
fields.description: An inferno of scarlet flames torches everything around the user. It may leave targets with a burn.

fields.name: techno-blast
fields.description: The user fires a beam of light at its target. The type changes depending on the Drive the user holds.

fields.name: relic-song
fields.description: The user sings an ancient song and attacks by appealing to the hearts of those listening. It may also induce sleep.

fields.name: secret-sword
fields.description: The user cuts with its long horn. The odd power contained in the horn does physical damage to the target.

fields.name: glaciate
fields.description: The user attacks by blowing freezing cold air at opposing Pokémon. This attack reduces the targets’ Speed stat.

fields.name: bolt-strike
fields.description: The user charges its target, surrounding itself with a great amount of electricity. It may leave the target with paralysis.

fields.name: blue-flare
fields.description: The user attacks by engulfing the target in an intense, yet beautiful, blue flame. It may leave the target with a burn.

fields.name: fiery-dance
fields.description: Cloaked in flames, the user dances and flaps its wings. It may also raise the user’s Sp. Atk stat.

fields.name: freeze-shock
fields.description: On the second turn, the user hits the target with electrically charged ice. It may leave the target with paralysis.

fields.name: ice-burn
fields.description: On the second turn, an ultracold, freezing wind surrounds the target. This may leave the target with a burn.

fields.name: snarl
fields.description: The user yells as if it is ranting about something, making the target’s Sp. Atk stat decrease.

fields.name: icicle-crash
fields.description: The user attacks by harshly dropping an icicle onto the target. It may also make the target flinch.

fields.name: v-create
fields.description: With a hot flame on its forehead, the user hurls itself at its target. It lowers the user’s Defense, Sp. Def, and Speed stats.

fields.name: fusion-flare
fields.description: The user brings down a giant flame. This attack does greater damage when influenced by an enormous thunderbolt.

fields.name: fusion-bolt
fields.description: The user throws down a giant thunderbolt. This attack does greater damage when influenced by an enormous flame.

fields.name: flying-press
fields.description: The user dives down onto the target from the sky. This move is Fighting and Flying type simultaneously.

fields.name: mat-block
fields.description: Using a pulled-up mat as a shield, the user protects itself and its allies from damaging moves. This does not stop status moves.

fields.name: belch
fields.description: The user lets out a damaging belch at the target. The user must eat a held Berry to use this move.

fields.name: rototiller
fields.description: Tilling the soil, the user makes it easier for plants to grow. This raises the Attack and Sp. Atk stats of Grass-type Pokémon.

fields.name: sticky-web
fields.description: The user weaves a sticky net around the opposing team, which lowers their Speed stat upon switching into battle.

fields.name: fell-stinger
fields.description: When the user knocks out a target with this move, the user’s Attack stat rises sharply.

fields.name: phantom-force
fields.description: The user vanishes somewhere, then strikes the target on the next turn. This move hits even if the target protects itself.

fields.name: trick-or-treat
fields.description: The user takes the target trick-or-treating. This adds Ghost type to the target’s type.

fields.name: noble-roar
fields.description: Letting out a noble roar, the user intimidates the target and lowers its Attack and Sp. Atk stats.

fields.name: ion-deluge
fields.description: The user disperses electrically charged particles, which changes Normal-type moves to Electric-type moves.

fields.name: parabolic-charge
fields.description: The user attacks everything around it. The user’s HP is restored by half the damage taken by those hit.

fields.name: forests-curse
fields.description: The user puts a forest curse on the target. Afflicted targets are now Grass type as well.

fields.name: petal-blizzard
fields.description: The user stirs up a violent petal blizzard and attacks everything around it.

fields.name: freeze-dry
fields.description: The user rapidly cools the target. This may also leave the target frozen. This move is super effective on Water types.

fields.name: disarming-voice
fields.description: Letting out a charming cry, the user does emotional damage to opposing Pokémon. This attack never misses.

fields.name: parting-shot
fields.description: With a parting threat, the user lowers the target’s Attack and Sp. Atk stats. Then it switches with a party Pokémon.

fields.name: topsy-turvy
fields.description: All stat changes affecting the target turn topsy-turvy and become the opposite of what they were.

fields.name: draining-kiss
fields.description: The user steals the target’s energy with a kiss. The user’s HP is restored by over half of the damage taken by the target.

fields.name: crafty-shield
fields.description: The user protects itself and its allies from status moves with a mysterious power. This does not stop moves that do damage.

fields.name: flower-shield
fields.description: The user raises the Defense stat of all Grass-type Pokémon in battle with a mysterious power.

fields.name: grassy-terrain
fields.description: The user turns the ground under everyone’s feet to grass for five turns. This restores the HP of Pokémon on the ground a little every turn.

fields.name: misty-terrain
fields.description: The user covers the ground under everyone’s feet with mist for five turns. This protects Pokémon on the ground from status conditions.

fields.name: electrify
fields.description: If the target is electrified before it uses a move during that turn, the target’s move becomes Electric type.

fields.name: play-rough
fields.description: The user plays rough with the target and attacks it. This may also lower the target’s Attack stat.

fields.name: fairy-wind
fields.description: The user stirs up a fairy wind and strikes the target with it.

fields.name: moonblast
fields.description: Borrowing the power of the moon, the user attacks the target. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Atk stat.

fields.name: boomburst
fields.description: The user attacks everything around it with the destructive power of a terrible, explosive sound.

fields.name: fairy-lock
fields.description: By locking down the battlefield, the user keeps all Pokémon from fleeing during the next turn.

fields.name: kings-shield
fields.description: The user takes a defensive stance while it protects itself from damage. It also harshly lowers the Attack stat of any attacker who makes direct contact.

fields.name: play-nice
fields.description: The user and the target become friends, and the target loses its will to fight. This lowers the target’s Attack stat.

fields.name: confide
fields.description: The user tells the target a secret, and the target loses its ability to concentrate. This lowers the target’s Sp. Atk stat.

fields.name: diamond-storm
fields.description: The user whips up a storm of diamonds to damage opposing Pokémon. This may also raise the user’s Defense stat.

fields.name: steam-eruption
fields.description: The user immerses the target in superheated steam. This may also leave the target with a burn.

fields.name: hyperspace-hole
fields.description: Using a hyperspace hole, the user appears right next to the target and strikes. This also hits a target using Protect or Detect.

fields.name: water-shuriken
fields.description: The user hits the target with throwing stars two to five times in a row. This move always goes first.

fields.name: mystical-fire
fields.description: The user attacks by breathing a special, hot fire. This also lowers the target’s Sp. Atk stat.

fields.name: spiky-shield
fields.description: In addition to protecting the user from attacks, this move also damages any attacker who makes direct contact.

fields.name: aromatic-mist
fields.description: The user raises the Sp. Def stat of an ally Pokémon by using a mysterious aroma.

fields.name: eerie-impulse
fields.description: The user’s body generates an eerie impulse. Exposing the target to it harshly lowers the target’s Sp. Atk stat.

fields.name: venom-drench
fields.description: Opposing Pokémon are drenched in an odd poisonous liquid. This lowers the Attack, Sp. Atk, and Speed stats of a poisoned target.

fields.name: powder
fields.description: The user covers the target in a powder that explodes and damages the target if it uses a Fire-type move.

fields.name: geomancy
fields.description: The user absorbs energy and sharply raises its Sp. Atk, Sp. Def, and Speed stats on the next turn.

fields.name: magnetic-flux
fields.description: The user manipulates magnetic fields, which raises the Defense and Sp. Def stats of ally Pokémon with the Plus or Minus Ability.

fields.name: happy-hour
fields.description: Using Happy Hour doubles the amount of prize money received after battle.

fields.name: electric-terrain
fields.description: The user electrifies the ground under everyone’s feet for five turns. Pokémon on the ground no longer fall asleep.

fields.name: dazzling-gleam
fields.description: The user damages opposing Pokémon by emitting a powerful flash.

fields.name: celebrate
fields.description: The Pokémon congratulates you on your special day!

fields.name: hold-hands
fields.description: The user and an ally hold hands. This makes them very happy.

fields.name: baby-doll-eyes
fields.description: The user stares at the target with its baby-doll eyes, which lowers its Attack stat. This move always goes first.

fields.name: nuzzle
fields.description: The user attacks by nuzzling its electrified cheeks against the target. This also leaves the target with paralysis.

fields.name: hold-back
fields.description: The user holds back when it attacks and the target is left with at least 1 HP.

fields.name: infestation
fields.description: The target is infested and attacked for four to five turns. The target can’t flee during this time.

fields.name: power-up-punch
fields.description: Striking opponents over and over makes the user’s fists harder. Hitting a target raises the Attack stat.

fields.name: oblivion-wing
fields.description: The user absorbs its target’s HP. The user’s HP is restored by over half of the damage taken by the target.

fields.name: thousand-arrows
fields.description: This move also hits opposing Pokémon that are in the air. Those Pokémon are knocked down to the ground.

fields.name: thousand-waves
fields.description: The user attacks with a wave that crawls along the ground. Those hit can’t flee from battle.

fields.name: lands-wrath
fields.description: The user gathers the energy of the land and focuses that power on opposing Pokémon to damage them.

fields.name: light-of-ruin
fields.description: Drawing power from the Eternal Flower, the user fires a powerful beam of light. This also damages the user quite a lot.

fields.name: origin-pulse
fields.description: The user attacks opposing Pokémon with countless beams of light that glow a deep and brilliant blue.

fields.name: precipice-blades
fields.description: The user attacks opposing Pokémon by manifesting the power of the land in fearsome blades of stone.

fields.name: dragon-ascent
fields.description: After soaring upward, the user attacks its target by dropping out of the sky at high speeds, although it lowers its own Defense and Sp. Def in the process.

fields.name: hyperspace-fury
fields.description: Using its many arms, the user unleashes a barrage of attacks that ignore the effects of moves like Protect and Detect. This attack lowers the user’s Defense.

fields.name: breakneck-blitz--physical
fields.description: The user builds up its momentum using its Z-Power and crashes into the target at full speed. The power varies, depending on the original move.

fields.name: breakneck-blitz--special
fields.description: Dummy Data

fields.name: all-out-pummeling--physical
fields.description: The user rams an energy orb created by its Z-Power into the target with full force. The power varies, depending on the original move.

fields.name: all-out-pummeling--special
fields.description: Dummy Data

fields.name: supersonic-skystrike--physical
fields.description: The user soars up with its Z-Power and plummets toward the target at full speed. The power varies, depending on the original move.

fields.name: supersonic-skystrike--special
fields.description: Dummy Data

fields.name: acid-downpour--physical
fields.description: The user creates a poisonous swamp using its Z-Power and sinks the target into it at full force. The power varies, depending on the original move.

fields.name: acid-downpour--special
fields.description: Dummy Data

fields.name: tectonic-rage--physical
fields.description: The user burrows deep into the ground and slams into the target with the full force of its Z-Power. The power varies, depending on the original move.

fields.name: tectonic-rage--special
fields.description: Dummy Data

fields.name: continental-crush--physical
fields.description: The user summons a huge rock mountain using its Z-Power and drops it onto the target with full force. The power varies, depending on the original move.

fields.name: continental-crush--special
fields.description: Dummy Data

fields.name: savage-spin-out--physical
fields.description: The user binds the target with full force with threads of silk that the user spits using its Z-Power. The power varies, depending on the original move.

fields.name: savage-spin-out--special
fields.description: Dummy Data

fields.name: never-ending-nightmare--physical
fields.description: Deep-seated grudges summoned by the user’s Z-Power trap the target. The power varies, depending on the original move.

fields.name: never-ending-nightmare--special
fields.description: Dummy Data

fields.name: corkscrew-crash--physical
fields.description: The user spins very fast and rams into the target with the full force of its Z-Power. The power varies, depending on the original move.

fields.name: corkscrew-crash--special
fields.description: Dummy Data

fields.name: floral-healing

fields.name: lunge

fields.name: fire-lash

fields.description: If the Pokémon the user has inflicted damage on have already used their moves, this move eliminates the effect of the target’s Ability.

fields.name: trop-kick

fields.name: instruct

fields.name: beak-blast

fields.name: clanging-scales

fields.name: brutal-swing

fields.name: aurora-veil

fields.name: malicious-moonsault

fields.description: The user slashes the target with aura-enriched wings. This also raises the user's action speed. This move has a heightened chance of landing a critical hit.

fields.name: bitter-malice
fields.description: The user attacks its target with spine-chilling resentment. This may also leave the target with frostbite. This move's power is doubled if the target has a status condition.

fields.name: shelter
fields.description: The user makes its skin as hard as an iron shield, raising its defensive stats. Incoming moves also become more likely to miss.

fields.name: triple-arrows
fields.description: The user delivers an axe kick, then fires three arrows. This raises the chance of its future attacks landing critical hits and also lowers the target's defensive stats.

fields.name: infernal-parade
fields.description: The user attacks with myriad fireballs. This may also leave the target with a burn. This move's power is doubled if the target has a status condition.

fields.name: ceaseless-edge
fields.description: The user slashes its shell blade at the target, aiming to land a critical hit. Shell splinters left behind by this attack will continue to damage the target for several turns.

fields.name: bleakwind-storm
fields.description: The user attacks with savagely cold winds that cause both body and spirit to tremble. This may also leave the target with frostbite.

fields.name: wildbolt-storm
fields.description: The user summons a thunderous tempest and savagely attacks with lightning and wind. This may also leave the target with paralysis.

fields.name: sandsear-storm
fields.description: The user attacks by wrapping the target in fierce winds and searingly hot sand. This also leaves the target with a burn.

fields.name: lunar-blessing
fields.description: The user heals its own status conditions and restores its HP. Incoming moves also become more likely to miss.

fields.name: take-heart
fields.description: The user lifts its spirits, healing its own status conditions and raising its offensive and defensive stats.

fields.name: tera-blast
fields.description: If the user has Terastallized, it unleashes energy of its Tera Type. This move inflicts damage using the Attack or Sp. Atk stat—whichever is higher for the user.

fields.name: silk-trap
fields.description: The user spins a silken trap, protecting itself from damage while lowering the Speed stat of any attacker that makes direct contact.

fields.name: axe-kick
fields.description: The user attacks by kicking up into the air and slamming its heel down upon the target. This may also confuse the target. If it misses, the user takes damage instead.

fields.name: last-respects
fields.description: The user attacks to avenge its allies. The more defeated allies there are in the user's party, the greater the move's power.

fields.name: lumina-crash
fields.description: The user attacks by unleashing a peculiar light that even affects the mind. This also harshly lowers the target's Sp. Def stat.

fields.name: order-up
fields.description: The user attacks with elegant poise. If the user has a Tatsugiri in its mouth, this move boosts one of the user's stats based on the Tatsugiri's form.

fields.name: jet-punch
fields.description: The user summons a torrent around its fist and punches at blinding speed. This move always goes first.

fields.name: spicy-extract
fields.description: The user emits an incredibly spicy extract, sharply boosting the target's Attack stat and harshly lowering the target's Defense stat.

fields.name: spin-out
fields.description: The user spins furiously by straining its legs, inflicting damage on the target. This also harshly lowers the user's Speed stat.

fields.name: population-bomb
fields.description: The user’s fellows gather in droves to perform a combo attack that hits the target one to ten times in a row.

fields.name: ice-spinner
fields.description: The user covers its feet in thin ice and twirls around, slamming into the target. This move's spinning motion also destroys the terrain.

fields.name: glaive-rush
fields.description: The user throws its entire body into a reckless charge. After this move is used, attacks on the user cannot miss and will inflict double damage until the user’s next turn.

fields.name: revival-blessing
fields.description: The user bestows a loving blessing, reviving a party Pokémon that has fainted and restoring half that Pokémon's max HP.

fields.name: salt-cure
fields.description: The user salt cures the target, inflicting damage every turn. Steel and Water types are more strongly affected by this move.

fields.name: triple-dive
fields.description: The user performs a perfectly timed triple dive, hitting the target with splashes of water three times in a row.

fields.name: mortal-spin
fields.description: The user performs a spin attack that can also eliminate the effects of such moves as Bind, Wrap, and Leech Seed. This also poisons opposing Pokémon.

fields.name: doodle
fields.description: The user captures the very essence of the target in a sketch. This changes the Abilities of the user and its ally Pokémon to that of the target.

fields.name: fillet-away
fields.description: The user sharply boosts its Attack, Sp. Atk, and Speed stats by using its own HP.

fields.name: kowtow-cleave
fields.description: The user slashes at the target after kowtowing to make the target let down its guard. This attack never misses.

fields.name: flower-trick
fields.description: The user throws a rigged bouquet of flowers at the target. This attack never misses and always lands a critical hit.

fields.name: torch-song
fields.description: The user blows out raging flames as if singing a song, scorching the target. This also boosts the user's Sp. Atk stat.

fields.name: aqua-step
fields.description: The user toys with the target and attacks it using light and fluid dance steps. This also boosts the user's Speed stat.

fields.name: raging-bull
fields.description: The user performs a tackle like a raging bull. This move's type depends on the user's form. It can also break barriers, such as Light Screen and Reflect.

fields.name: make-it-rain
fields.description: The user attacks by throwing out a mass of coins. This also lowers the user's Sp. Atk stat. Money is earned after the battle.

fields.name: psyblade
fields.description: The user rends the target with an ethereal blade. This move's power is boosted by 50 percent if the user is on Electric Terrain.

fields.name: hydro-steam
fields.description: The user blasts the target with boiling-hot water. This move's power is not lowered in harsh sunlight but rather boosted by 50 percent.

fields.name: ruination
fields.description: The user summons a ruinous disaster. This cuts the target’s HP in half.

fields.name: collision-course
fields.description: The user transforms and crashes to the ground, causing a massive prehistoric explosion. This move's power is boosted more than usual if it’s a supereffective hit.

fields.name: electro-drift
fields.description: The user races forward at ultrafast speeds, piercing its target with futuristic electricity. This move's power is boosted more than usual if it's a supereffective hit.

fields.name: shed-tail
fields.description: The user creates a substitute for itself using its own HP before switching places with a party Pokémon in waiting.

fields.name: chilly-reception
fields.description: No description.

fields.name: tidy-up
fields.description: The user tidies up and removes the effects of Spikes, Stealth Rock, Sticky Web, Toxic Spikes, and Substitute. This also boosts the user’s Attack and Speed stats.

fields.name: snowscape
fields.description: The user summons a snowstorm lasting five turns. This boosts the Defense stats of Ice types.

fields.name: pounce
fields.description: The user attacks by pouncing on the target. This also lowers the target's Speed stat.

fields.name: trailblaze
fields.description: The user attacks suddenly as if leaping out from tall grass. The user's nimble footwork boosts its Speed stat.

fields.name: chilling-water
fields.description: The user attacks the target by showering it with water that's so cold it saps the target's power. This also lowers the target's Attack stat.

fields.name: hyper-drill
fields.description: The user spins the pointed part of its body at high speed to pierce the target. This attack can hit a target using a move such as Protect or Detect.

fields.name: twin-beam
fields.description: The user shoots mystical beams from its eyes to inflict damage. The target is hit twice in a row.

fields.name: rage-fist
fields.description: The user converts its rage into energy to attack. The more times the user has been hit by attacks, the greater the move's power.

fields.name: armor-cannon
fields.description: The user shoots its own armor out as blazing projectiles. This also lowers the user’s Defense and Sp. Def stats.

fields.name: bitter-blade
fields.description: The user focuses its bitter feelings toward the world of the living into a slashing attack. The user's HP is restored by up to half the damage taken by the target.

fields.name: double-shock
fields.description: The user discharges all the electricity from its body to perform a high-damage attack. After using this move, the user will no longer be Electric type.

fields.name: gigaton-hammer
fields.description: The user swings its whole body around to attack with its huge hammer. This move can't be used twice in a row.

fields.name: comeuppance
fields.description: The user retaliates with much greater force against the opponent that last inflicted damage on it.

fields.name: aqua-cutter
fields.description: The user expels pressurized water to cut at the target like a blade. This move has a heightened chance of landing a critical hit.

fields.name: blazing-torque
fields.description: No description.

fields.name: wicked-torque
fields.description: No description.

fields.name: noxious-torque
fields.description: No description.

fields.name: combat-torque
fields.description: No description.

fields.name: magical-torque
fields.description: No description.

fields.name: blood-moon
fields.description: The user unleashes the full brunt of its spirit from a full moon that shines as red as blood. This move can't be used twice in a row.

fields.name: matcha-gotcha
fields.description: The user fires a blast of tea that it mixed. The user's HP is restored by up to half the damage taken by the target. This may also leave the target with a burn.

fields.name: syrup-bomb
fields.description: The user sets off an explosion of sticky candy syrup, which coats the target and causes the target's Speed stat to drop each turn for three turns.

fields.name: ivy-cudgel
fields.description: The user strikes with an ivy-wrapped cudgel. This move's type changes depending on the mask worn by the user, and it has a heightened chance of landing a critical hit.

fields.name: electro-shot
fields.description: The user gathers electricity on the first turn, boosting its Sp. Atk stat, then fires a high-voltage shot on the next turn. The shot will be fired immediately in rain.

fields.name: tera-starstorm
fields.description: With the power of its crystals, the user bombards and eliminates the target. When used by Terapagos in its Stellar Form, this move damages all opposing Pokémon.

fields.name: fickle-beam
fields.description: The user shoots a beam of light to inflict damage. Sometimes all the user's heads shoot beams in unison, doubling the move's power.

fields.name: burning-bulwark
fields.description: The user's intensely hot fur protects it from attacks and also burns any attacker that makes direct contact with it.

fields.name: thunderclap
fields.description: This move enables the user to attack first with a jolt of electricity. This move fails if the target is not readying an attack.

fields.name: mighty-cleave
fields.description: The user wields the light that has accumulated atop its head to cleave the target. This move hits even if the target protects itself.

fields.name: tachyon-cutter
fields.description: The user attacks by launching particle blades at the target twice in a row. This attack never misses.

fields.name: hard-press
fields.description: The target is crushed with an arm, a claw, or the like to inflict damage. The more HP the target has left, the greater the move's power.

fields.name: dragon-cheer
fields.description: The user raises its allies’ morale with a draconic cry so that their future attacks have a heightened chance of landing critical hits. This rouses Dragon types more.

fields.name: alluring-voice
fields.description: The user attacks the target using its angelic voice. This also confuses the target if its stats have been boosted during the turn.

fields.name: temper-flare
fields.description: Spurred by desperation, the user attacks the target. This move's power is doubled if the user's previous move failed.

fields.name: supercell-slam
fields.description: The user electrifies its body and drops onto the target to inflict damage. If this move misses, the user takes damage instead.

fields.name: psychic-noise
fields.description: The user attacks the target with unpleasant sound waves. For two turns, the target is prevented from recovering HP through moves, Abilities, or held items.

fields.name: upper-hand
fields.description: The user reacts to the target's movement and strikes with the heel of its palm, making the target flinch. This move fails if the target is not readying a priority move.

fields.name: malignant-chain
fields.description: The user pours toxins into the target by wrapping them in a toxic, corrosive chain. This may also leave the target badly poisoned.
